description: A Joe Lee-designed executive course nestled inside the Barefoot Bay Recreation District, this 18-hole, par-60 layout is owned and operated by residents of the surrounding community. At under 3,700 yards it rewards precision over power, making it a regular haunt for the area's active adult population.

notes: Skipped per rules — driving range only

description: Operated by the City of Cocoa Beach, this 27-hole facility sits on a peninsula bracketed by the Banana River Lagoon and offers three distinct nine-hole combinations. The courses were reconfigured in the early 1990s, giving the layout a distinctly modern Florida feel while remaining one of the most affordable rounds on the Space Coast.

Designed by Jim Fazio and opened in 1991, Fairwinds earned a 4.5-star rating from Golf Digest's Places to Play, a distinction rarely given to daily-fee courses on the Treasure Coast. The 18-hole layout winds through mature vegetation along the North Fork of the St. Lucie River, with water in play on the majority of holes.

An Arthur Hills design that has operated as a semi-private club since opening in 1986, Gator Trace plays to a par 70 across 6,136 yards with water incorporated on every hole. The course offers daily-fee play alongside annual and seasonal memberships, keeping its fees among the most accessible in St. Lucie County.

One of Fort Pierce's oldest courses, Island Pines opened in 1970 and offers an 18-hole public layout that remains a local favorite for year-round play. Annual, seasonal, and range memberships are available alongside open tee times, giving the course a community-club character without a mandatory membership.

Indian Hills is a city-owned municipal course that traces its origins to 1942, making it one of the oldest public layouts on Florida's Treasure Coast. Located at 1600 S 3rd Street in Fort Pierce, the course is operated by the City and offers some of the most affordable green fees in St. Lucie County.

The Harbor Course at Grand Harbor Golf and Beach Club is a Pete Dye design that opened in 1989, distinguished by tiered greens, deep pot bunkers, and a links-style aesthetic uncommon in South Florida. The private club also features a second course — the River Course by Joe Lee — and sits on the Indian River in the unincorporated community of Gifford.
notes: Physical address uses Vero Beach, FL 32967; property is in the Gifford area of Indian River County

description: Pointe West Country Club is a private, Luna Golf-managed club in Vero Beach offering an 18-hole championship layout and membership by invitation. The course opened around 1999 inside a gated residential community on the west side of Indian River County, providing a quieter alternative to the barrier-island clubs nearby.
notes: Listed as Indian River County; actual city is Vero Beach

description: Designed by Ted McAnlis and opened in 1984, Indianwood is a par-70, 18-hole public course set within a 55+ manufactured home community in Indiantown. At just over 6,000 yards the layout is approachable for all skill levels and one of the few open-to-the-public courses in inland Martin County.

The Ocean Club is an 18-hole executive course at the Marriott Hutchinson Island Beach Resort, designed by Charles Ankrom and opened in 1978. Playing to a par of 61, it is the most accessible course on Hutchinson Island and the only golf option at a full-service oceanfront resort on the Treasure Coast.
notes: Listed as Martin County; actual city is Stuart (555 NE Ocean Blvd, Stuart FL 34996)
Unknown course — YCS Martin County; no identifiable golf facility found matching this name or acronym in Martin County

description: Martin Downs Country Club in Palm City features 27 holes of championship golf — the Banyan Creek, Osprey Creek, and Crane Creek nines, all designed by Charles Ankrom. Developed in the late 1970s, it operates as a semi-private resort club within the Martin Downs gated community.
notes: Listed as Martin County; actual city is Palm City

description: Heritage Ridge in Hobe Sound opened its front nine in 1980 and completed the back nine in 1982, giving it a split-design lineage with Dom Fazio credited as the shaping architect. The 18-hole layout plays through classic Florida parkland terrain and is open to the public with daily-fee access.
notes: Listed as Martin County; actual city is Hobe Sound

description: One of Florida's oldest public courses, Sailfish Sands traces its roots to the St. Lucie River Country Club, whose first nine holes opened in January 1925 to a William Langford design. County-owned since 1947 and renamed Sailfish Sands following a 2020 renovation by John Sanford, it remains the flagship public golf destination in Martin County.
notes: Listed as Martin County; located adjacent to Stuart at 2000 SE Saint Lucie Blvd, Stuart FL 34996

description: Designed by Tom Fazio and opened in 1993, The Champions Club at Summerfield consistently ranks among the best public courses on Florida's Treasure Coast. The 18-hole layout measures nearly 6,900 yards from the tips and is set within the Summerfield residential development in Stuart.
notes: Listed as Martin County; actual city is Stuart

description: Crane Creek Reserve is one of Florida's most historically significant municipal courses, originating as the Melbourne Golf and Country Club in 1920 and expanded to 18 holes under a Donald Ross design that debuted in December 1928. Bill Amick renovated the layout extensively in the late 1970s, and it continues to operate as a public, daily-fee facility for the City of Melbourne.

Harbor City is a Bill Amick-designed municipal course opened in 1966, operated by the City of Melbourne and formerly known as Mallard Landing. Following a city-funded renovation the course was rebranded Harbor City, and it remains one of Melbourne's two public municipal golf facilities.

Indian River Colony Club is a 55+ private military retirement community in Viera whose Gordon G. Lewis-designed course opened in 1989. Membership is open to any honorably discharged veteran and, to a limited extent, non-military members — giving the club a distinct identity as one of the few golf communities in Brevard County built specifically to serve those who served.
notes: Private club community for military veterans and retirees

description: Duran Golf Club opened in 2005 in Viera, designed by Chip Powell with a links-style profile that stands apart from the typical tree-lined Florida layout. Consistently rated the top public course in Brevard County, the club also hosts the area's only lighted nine-hole par-3 course and a nationally ranked practice facility.

Suntree Country Club features two 18-hole courses — the Classic (1975) and the Suntree course designed by Arnold Palmer (1989) — within a private, member-owned community in Melbourne. Access is restricted to members and their guests, with limited reciprocal privileges for Brevard County residents.

Aquarina Beach and Country Club features a Charles Ankrom-designed 18-hole executive course that opened in 1996, playing to a par of 62 along the Atlantic coast of Melbourne Beach. The private club is part of a gated residential community and pairs golf with beach access on one of Brevard County's less-developed barrier island stretches.

Spessard Holland Golf Course opened in 1977 on a narrow barrier island strip between the Atlantic Ocean and the Indian River, designed by Arnold Palmer, Ed Seay, and Robert Walker. The par-67 public layout is operated by Brevard County through its Golf Brevard program alongside The Habitat in Malabar.

The Habitat is a Charles Ankrom-designed 18-hole public course that opened in 1991 in Malabar, operated by Golf Brevard, a non-profit managing it alongside Spessard Holland. The course sits in a nature-rich setting near Valkaria, blending maintained fairways with Florida scrub habitat.
notes: Listed as Melbourne Beach; physical location is Malabar/Valkaria (3591 Fairgreen St, Malabar FL 32950)

description: The Savannahs Golf Course — now rebranded as Space Coast Golf Club — opened in 1989–1990 on Merritt Island and plays through an open, links-influenced layout at 3915 Savannahs Trail. The public course has long been a budget-friendly go-to for Space Coast golfers looking for a flat, fast round.

Indian River Preserve opened in 2003 under the name Walkabout Golf Club before rebranding, and sits just off I-95 in Mims near Titusville. The 18-hole public course offers a semi-remote round in a natural preserve setting, drawing golfers from both Brevard and Volusia counties.

Hammock Creek Golf Club is a Jack Nicklaus and Jack Nicklaus II Legacy Design that opened in 1996, set along natural preserves in Palm City. The semi-private club offers public tee times alongside a full-service bag drop, golf shop, and restaurant, and the course is widely considered one of the best values on the Treasure Coast.

The Golden Marsh is one of two 18-hole championship courses at the private Harbour Ridge Yacht and Country Club in Palm City, designed by Joe Lee and opened in 1984. Bobby Weed completed a renovation of the course in 2018, refreshing its signature routing through natural lakes and sawgrass marshes along the St. Lucie River.
notes: This is a named course at Harbour Ridge Yacht & Country Club, not a standalone facility

description: The River Ridge Course is the second championship layout at Harbour Ridge Yacht and Country Club, designed by Pete Dye and P.B. Dye and opened in 2015. It carries one of the highest slope ratings in Florida, playing 6,668 yards across bluffs that drop toward the St. Lucie River.
notes: This is a named course at Harbour Ridge Yacht & Country Club, not a standalone facility

description: Piper's Landing Yacht and Country Club is a member-owned private community on the Treasure Coast whose golf course opened in 1981, stretching to 6,922 yards across 600 acres alongside the St. Lucie River. With just 302 home sites and no outside play, the club delivers an intimate, unhurried round unavailable elsewhere in Martin County.

The Evergreen Club is a semi-private 18-hole course designed by Charlie Martin and opened in 1978, spread across 520 acres in northeastern Martin County. Golf membership is optional, not mandatory, so daily-fee players and members coexist — a layout now operating as Crane Watch Club.
notes: Now operating as Crane Watch Club

description: Monarch Country Club is an Arnold Palmer and Ed Seay-designed 18-hole course opened in 1987, now managed within the Invited Clubs network. The private club in Palm City pairs championship golf with tennis, pool, and dining amenities, and offers memberships to residents and non-residents alike.

Originally opened as Cobblestone Country Club in 1989 and later rebranded as The Fox Club, the course was acquired and relaunched in late 2022 as the Cape Club of Palm City, a private members-only facility affiliated with two Massachusetts sister clubs. The 18-hole layout at 10664 SW Whooping Crane Way is one of the newer private entrants on the Martin County golf scene.
notes: Now operating as Cape Club of Palm City (private) since 2022; formerly The Fox Club (semi-private)

description: Originally developed in 1996 for Wayne Huizenga as a personal retreat with virtually no outside membership, The Floridian was purchased by Jim Crane in 2010 and redesigned by Tom Fazio before reopening in 2011. With fewer than 30 members, the club on the St. Lucie River remains one of the most exclusive private facilities in the United States.
notes: Ultra-exclusive private club with approximately 29 members; original design by Gary Player (1996), redesigned by Tom Fazio (reopened 2011)

description: Manatee Cove is a Robert Trent Jones Sr.-designed 18-hole course that opened around 1962 on Patrick Space Force Base, occupying 260 acres between the Banana River and the Atlantic Ocean. Access is extended to active-duty military, veterans, retirees, and DoD civilians with base access, as well as sponsored civilian guests.
notes: Military course on Patrick Space Force Base; open to military personnel and eligible guests

description: Trident Golf Club — formerly Sandpiper Bay Golf Club — is an 18-hole public course designed by Mark Mahannah that opened in 1963 at the Club Med Sandpiper Bay resort in Port St. Lucie. The course features a modern performance center and is open for daily-fee play for both resort guests and outside visitors.

Miles Grant Country Club opened in 1972 on the shores of the Intracoastal Waterway in the Stuart area, offering a private 18-hole layout in a quiet residential community. The course plays along East Cove Road in Port Salerno and carries the 34997 Stuart zip code.

Mariner Sands Country Club was planned in 1979 and opened in the early 1980s across 720 acres in Stuart, operating as a private gated community. The club features a White Heron 18-hole championship course and a shorter Gold course, both maintained to high standards for its resident members.
notes: Mailing address is Stuart FL 34997 though community is near Port Salerno

description: The Saints at Port St. Lucie is a municipal par-72 course designed by David Wallace that opened in 1961, making it one of the oldest public layouts in St. Lucie County. At 6,478 yards the course is walkable and among the most affordable daily-fee options in the Port St. Lucie area.

Saint Lucie Trail Golf Club is a Jim Fazio-designed 18-hole public course that opened in 1989 and was formerly known as PGA Country Club and St. Lucie West. A comprehensive greens renovation was completed in summer 2024, refreshing the layout that plays from 951 SW Country Club Drive in Port St. Lucie.

PGA Golf Club at PGA Village is the PGA of America's home complex, offering 54 holes designed by Tom Fazio (Ryder and Wanamaker courses, 1996) and Pete Dye (Dye Course, 2000). A public resort destination ranked among Golf Digest's 75 Best Golf Resorts, the facility also houses the PGA Learning Center — one of the largest golf instruction campuses in the world.
notes: PGA of America's home facility; 54-hole public resort complex

description: Tesoro Club features two signature 18-hole courses — the Arnold Palmer Course (2005) and the Tom Watson Course (2008) — inside a gated private community in Port St. Lucie. After the club fully closed in 2021, the Palmer Course was renovated and reopened in 2022, marking a new chapter for one of the Treasure Coast's most ambitious private developments.

Rockledge Country Club is one of Brevard County's oldest clubs, with an official opening in early 1925 and roots stretching back to 1918. The semi-private layout at 1591 Fiske Blvd is listed on the Florida Historic Golf Trail, balancing member privileges with public tee time availability.

Turtle Creek Golf Club opened in 1971 and operated as a public course in Rockledge for 52 years before closing on May 31, 2025, when the owners retired. The 119-acre property has been sold for residential development, ending one of the longest-running public golf operations in Brevard County.
notes: CLOSED May 31, 2025 — course sold for residential development

description: Viera East Golf Club opened in 1994 in the master-planned Viera community and has become one of the most-played public courses in Brevard County. The 18-hole layout at 2300 Clubhouse Drive is consistently well-reviewed for its conditioning and pace of play.

RedStick Golf Club opened in November 2000 as a private, non-residential club designed by Rees Jones and Greg Muirhead in Vero Beach. With no tee times, caddie-preferred play, and a membership-by-invitation policy, RedStick operates on the model of classic private clubs and appears on lists of the top 100 courses in the United States.
notes: Listed as Sebastian; physical address is 8350 58th Ave, Vero Beach FL 32967 (Indian River County)

description: John's Island Club was founded in 1969 and its West Course — a Tom Fazio design — opened in 1988 on the barrier island south of Vero Beach near Sebastian. The private club is considered one of the finest residential golf communities in Florida, with restricted access exclusively for members and invited guests.
notes: Listed as Sebastian; John's Island Club is in Indian River Shores/Vero Beach area; address 5804 87th St uses Sebastian zip 32958 but club is functionally Vero Beach

description: Sebastian Municipal Golf Course is an 18-hole public course designed by Charles Ankrom that opened in 1981 on a 155-acre site adjacent to Sebastian Municipal Airport. The layout underwent a major renovation in 2001, and at 6,717 yards and par 72 it remains the best daily-fee value in southern Indian River County.

Willoughby Golf Club is a private, member-owned community in Stuart whose 18-hole course opened in 1988. The gated development at 3001 SE Doubleton Drive has earned a reputation for consistent conditioning in the heart of Martin County's most sought-after residential corridor.

Sailfish Point Golf Club opened in 1981 as the ninth Jack Nicklaus Signature course ever built, stretching 7,117 yards along the southern tip of Hutchinson Island where the Atlantic Ocean meets the St. Lucie Inlet. A Nicklaus Design renovation completed in December 2021 brought the course back to its original intent, and access is strictly limited to members of this oceanfront private community.

The Florida Club is a public 18-hole course that opened in 1996 in Stuart, offering par-72 golf at 6,738 yards with no mandatory membership. Located at 9005 SW Old Royal Drive, it draws a loyal daily-fee following as one of the more polished public options in Martin County.

Monterey Yacht and Country Club features a P.B. Dye-designed 9-hole executive course built in 1970, playing to a par of 30 across just over 1,200 yards within a 55+ condominium community in Stuart. Access is private and reserved for residents, who sign up informally whenever they wish to play.
notes: 9-hole executive course; 55+ private residential community

description: Sherwood Golf Club opened as a 9-hole course in 1965 and expanded to 18 holes in 1973, serving Titusville as a public facility for decades. The course closed in 2019 and Brevard County commissioners approved a housing development on the 130-acre property in September 2024.
notes: CLOSED 2019 — property approved for residential redevelopment in 2024

description: Vista Plantation Golf Club is a semi-private Arthur Hills-designed par-62 course that opened in 1986 along State Route 60 in Vero Beach, convenient to the Indian River Mall. The 18-hole layout welcomes public play and offers membership options, covering a compact but challenging executive-adjacent course.

Hawk's Nest Golf Club is a Jim Fazio-designed private course that opened in 1987 and is affiliated with The Moorings Yacht and Country Club in Vero Beach. The private layout at 6005 Old Dixie Highway offers members a quieter, non-residential club experience separate from the Moorings residential community.

Indian River Club is a Ron Garl-designed 18-hole private course that opened in 1995 on 300 acres in Vero Beach, earning Audubon Signature Sanctuary certification — the only course with that designation in Indian River County. The private, member-owned club sits on the western side of Vero Beach away from the barrier island clubs.

Vero Beach Country Club is the oldest private golf club in Vero Beach, with its Herbert Strong-designed 18-hole course dating to 1924. Located at 800 30th Street, the club operates on a members-and-reciprocal basis and remains the historic anchor of private golf in Indian River County.

American Golf Club is a 27-hole public facility in Vero Beach comprising three nine-hole courses — the Gator, Heron, and Eagle — all designed by Arthur Hills and opened in 2012. The family-friendly layout at 100 Woodland Drive fills a gap in affordable public golf on the south side of Vero Beach.

Orchid Island Golf and Beach Club features a 1990 Arnold Palmer and Ed Seay-designed course certified as an Audubon Cooperative Sanctuary, winding through 375 home sites on a barrier island north of Vero Beach. Fully private and member-owned, the club combines a mile of Atlantic beachfront with a championship layout that is completely off-limits to non-members.

Baytree National Golf Links is a Gary Player-designed 18-hole public course that opened in 1992 in the Viera community of Melbourne, regarded as one of the best public tests in Brevard County. The layout at 8207 National Drive rewards strategic play and has consistently received top marks from Florida golf guides.
notes: Address uses Melbourne FL 32940; located in Viera community

description: Windsor Club was designed by Robert Trent Jones Jr. and opened in 1992 within the Windsor private sporting and residential community on Vero Beach's barrier island. The 7,068-yard, par-72 layout is part of a 472-acre estate that includes tennis, equestrian, and beach facilities, with membership and residency tightly controlled.
notes: Listed as Windsor; physical location is 3125 Windsor Blvd, Vero Beach FL 32963
